description_long: This object consists of two parallel steel rails mounted on a series of evenly spaced concrete sleepers, forming a short segment of standard railway track. The rails are metallic, slightly weathered, and rest securely on the sleepers, which are rectangular and have a rough, gray surface. The rails are fastened to the sleepers with visible metal plates and bolts, ensuring stability and alignment. There is no visible ballast bed beneath or around the sleepers, and the track segment appears to be isolated, not embedded in pavement. The structure is robust and designed to support the heavy loads of passing trains or trams. No signage, electrical components, or additional features are present.
description: A short section of railway track with two parallel steel rails mounted on gray concrete sleepers, showing a sturdy and industrial appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two parallel steel rails and the tops of three concrete sleepers beneath them.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the length of the rails and the rectangular shape of the concrete sleepers.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the rails and sleepers from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Angled view from the rear, emphasizing the alignment and spacing of the rails and sleepers.
